description: An obvious large, dark dabbling duck in southeast part of reservoir, the bird resembled MALL in overall shape and size.  During our entire observeration, the bird foraged in shallow water, often near an apparent brood of MALL-like ducks. Steve first noted it and got me looking at it. The bill's ground color was a bit hard to determine -- somewhere in the yellowy-orange range -- and had significant dusky markings. The crown was noticeably darker than the face and was separated from a short, dark eyeline by pale brown supercilium.  The rest of the face was notably buffy, but with some vague streaking on the cheek.  I could not discern the presence of a distinct black gape patch due to the distance, though one could certainly have been present undetected.  The nape was only marginally darker than the face, while the foreneck was buffy-gray, contrasting rather sharply with the darker chest.  The upperparts were very dark brown, noticeably much darker than the apparent brood of MALL youngsters (which are typically darker than adult female MALLs in this regard).  The tail was dark, just a bit paler than the upperparts, but obviously with no distinct white bits.  The underparts were of similar coloration to the upperparts, including the under-tail coverts, which were not at all pale.  The bird did not open its wings during our observation, but at one point I could see the speculum somewhat well and noted only a very thin white trailing edge, no other white being visible on the wing.
